---
title: "Assessment of walleye pollock in the eastern Bering Sea"
subtitle: "Working draft for 2026"
author:
  - name: "TODO: lead assessment author"
  - name: "TODO: coauthors"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Working assessment status"}
This draft supports an early September 2026 review and a final November 2026
assessment. Several annual inputs are pending. Values and conclusions must not
be carried into the executive summary until their source, review milestone, and
validation status are recorded in the annual manifests.
:::

# Executive Summary

## Summary of changes in assessment inputs

The last accepted assessment was completed in 2024. No operational assessment
was completed in 2025. The 2026 assessment will incorporate validated new data
available for the September and November review milestones. TODO: summarize the
final data additions and identify preliminary or unavailable components.

## Changes in the data

TODO: populate from `data-manifest.csv` after the September data freeze. Separate
new fishery, BTS, ATS, AVO, FT-NIRS, and ecosystem information.

## Changes in assessment methods

The 2026 bridge begins from the accepted 2024 Model 23 configuration. Work
conducted during 2025 in response to the CIE review will be introduced
sequentially after its exact source and configuration are confirmed. TODO: list
each method change and its effect relative to the data-only bridge.

## Summary of results

TODO: add results only after the candidate base model, diagnostics, and
projections are validated.

## Responses to SSC, Plan Team, and CIE comments

TODO: separate CIE responses, recurring general comments, and stock-specific
Plan Team or SSC comments. Link every response to evidence.

# Introduction

TODO: update only material that changed since the 2024 accepted assessment and
cite that assessment for stable background.

# Fishery and Management History

TODO: update catch, ABC, TAC, OFL, fishery distribution, and bycatch through the
confirmed terminal years.

# Data

## Data readiness

The current inventory is maintained in `data-manifest.csv`. Candidate files are
not final inputs until their provider, version, units, processing, and review
status are confirmed.

## Fishery data and FT-NIRS ages

TODO: document catch-at-age, weight-at-age, sampling, strata, and the treatment
and validation of FT-NIRS ages.

# Survey Data

TODO: document BTS, directed acoustic-trawl, and AVO delivery versions, survey
timing, coverage, uncertainty, age compositions, and processing changes.

# Other Data

TODO: distinguish contextual ecosystem information from data entering the model
likelihood or projections.

# Analytic Approach

## Bridge models

The bridge sequence is: 2024 reproduction; September 2025 post-CIE development
reconstruction; 2026 data-only update; and sequential addition of approved
method changes. TODO: report objective values and key outputs at every step.

## Candidate base model and alternatives

TODO: define the September and November candidate models and the rationale for
each alternative.

# Results

TODO: add estimates, data fits, convergence checks, profiles, retrospectives,
MCMC diagnostics, and model comparisons.

# Projections and Harvest Recommendations

TODO: document the terminal-state export, weight-at-age treatment, recruitment
assumptions, standard scenarios, OFL, maximum permissible ABC, and recommended
ABC.

# Risk Table and ABC Recommendation

TODO: complete after the evidence and projection results are final.

# Area Allocation of ABC

TODO: report the applicable method or state why this section is not applicable.

# Status Determination

TODO: report overfishing, overfished, and approaching-overfished determinations.

# Flimit

TODO: complete according to the 2026 guideline requirement.

# Ecosystem Considerations

TODO: summarize decision-relevant ecosystem information without conflating
contextual evidence with model inputs.

# Data Gaps and Research Priorities

TODO: update from the CIE review and subsequent work.

# Acknowledgements

TODO.

# Literature Cited

TODO: connect the assessment bibliography.

# Auxiliary Files

TODO: link the final data, model, projection, diagnostics, and review bundles.

# Tables

TODO: generate from versioned machine-readable outputs.

# Figures

TODO: generate from versioned machine-readable outputs and provide alt text.

# Appendices and Working Papers

TODO: link post-CIE working papers and detailed reproducibility material.
